name: Chrono Timezone MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for converting and comparing dates and times across any timezone with flexible, locale-aware formatting. Enables AI agents to handle timezone conversions, calculate time differences, schedule across timezones, and format datetime values for international workflows — solving the common timezone confusion in global team contexts.
use_cases: Converting meeting times across timezones for scheduling agents
Calculating time differences and duration for time-aware workflow agents
Formatting dates and times in locale-appropriate formats from international agents
Resolving timezone ambiguity in global team communication from assistant agents
not_for: High-precision timestamp operations for financial/legal applications
Complex business day calculations with holiday calendars
best_when: An AI assistant or scheduling agent needs reliable timezone conversion and datetime formatting — enabling accurate cross-timezone scheduling and time communication for global teams.
avoid_when: You need calendar integration or complex business day calculations. For simple timezone math, standard library functions may suffice without an MCP.

auth.notes: No auth required. Local timezone computation using standard time libraries.

pricing.notes: Free open source. No external service required.

known_agent_gotchas: Timezone database must be current — outdated tzdata can cause DST errors
Ambiguous times (DST transitions) need careful handling
Locale formatting options vary — test for your target locales
Simple utility tool — well-scoped with low risk of misuse
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local computation. No external data transmission. No auth required. Very low security risk.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local timezone computation. No external data. No auth. Minimal security risk.
